meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Master SaaS Development for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap in your SaaS knowledge using Git and AI. An intermediate guide for freelancers to ship faster using Antigravity and Claude Code workflows.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of deep SaaS architecture knowledge, leverage Git as your primary source of truth while using AI agents to scaffold complex logic. By integrating Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you transform Git commits into a structured, production-grade development workflow that scales.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer’s greatest adversary. You have the vision for a high-converting SaaS, but the labyrinth of backend architecture, state management, and database schema design feels like a wall you cannot climb. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking the industrial-grade leverage that turns hours of boilerplate into minutes of execution.

Today, we dismantle that wall. We are moving beyond simple code generation into a professional Git-first workflow that treats your repository as an intelligent partner. By combining the architectural precision of Antigravity with the raw coding power of Claude Code, you will ship complex features faster than your clients can even define their requirements.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Define your SaaS requirements in Antigravity to generate a robust project blueprint before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular Next.js SaaS architecture blueprint including authentication, Stripe integration, and a PostgreSQL schema. Focus on clean, maintainable code structures.

steps.title: Branch-Driven Development
steps.description: Isolate every feature into its own Git branch to ensure your AI-generated code remains modular and easy to roll back.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of git branch naming conventions and atomic task descriptions for a typical SaaS user dashboard feature.

steps.title: Context-Aware Coding
steps.description: Feed your existing codebase context into Claude Code to ensure new features align with your project's architectural standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refine my current database schema to handle multi-tenant isolation based on the previous architecture blueprint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement user dashboard UI using Tailwind and existing API hooks'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Use AI to scan your diffs for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks before merging into the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a senior engineer code review on these changes, specifically checking for SQL injection risks and inefficient database queries.

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Integration
steps.description: Automate your Git workflow so every merge triggers a deployment, keeping your client's staging environment live at all times.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file for automated testing and deployment of this repository to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement deployment automation' && git push origin feature/user-dashboard

pro_tips: Always commit after every successful AI task completion; this creates a granular history that allows you to 'time travel' if an AI suggestion introduces a regression.
Use a .claudeignore file to prevent the AI from indexing massive node_modules or build folders, saving token costs and improving context relevance.
When Claude Code gets stuck on a complex bug, ask it to 'explain its reasoning' before asking for a fix; it often catches its own logic errors.
Treat your Git commit messages as documentation for your client; use clear, functional language that proves you are building a professional-grade product.
If the AI outputs monolithic files, explicitly prompt it to 'refactor into smaller, single-responsibility components' to maintain a clean Git history.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, audit your current project repositories and implement a standard .claudeignore and README template to ensure your future development remains consistent across every client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the learning curve and start shipping like a senior architect. Every day you wait is a day of lost revenue and wasted engineering hours. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
